en.extract:
The banded wattle-eye (Platysteira laticincta) is a species of bird in the family Platysteiridae. It is endemic to the Bamenda Highlands in western Cameroon. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. In particular, it likely prefers low-altitude forests with more bare ground and denser undergrowth cover.
It is threatened by habitat loss.

The '''banded wattle-eye''' ('''''Platysteira laticincta''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Platysteiridae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Bamenda Highlands]] in western [[Cameroon]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. In particular, it likely prefers low-[[altitude]] [[forest]]s with more bare ground and denser [[Understory|undergrowth]] cover.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=A. |first1=Valery Binda |last2=Mayaka |first2=Theodore B. |date=January 2019 |title=Occupancy and Conservation Prospects of Endemic Banded Wattle-Eye ''Platysteira laticincta'' in the Kilum–Ijim Community Forest, Northwestern Cameroon |journal=Tropical Conservation Science |volume=12 |page=194008291988288 |doi=10.1177/1940082919882884 |issn=1940-0829|doi-access=free }}</ref>

It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
